rulings.Case Title: PHILLIP LOZANO VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for January 26, 2017, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Patricia Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Chandler Lumber delivered potentially asbestos-containing products to his jobsites. Plaintiff's deposition testimony precludes defendant from sustaining its burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of presenting evidence that conclusively negates an element of plaintiff's claim. Further, evidence submitted by defendant - the deposition testimony of plaintiff and Marc Myers - creates a triable issue of material fact whether Chandler Lumber delivered non-lumber products to plaintiff's jobsites, which precludes summary judgment. This was the sole issue properly raised by defendant's moving papers. Plaintiff's objections to Mr. Myers' deposition testimony are overruled as to objection no. 1 and sustained as to objection nos. 2 and 3 as to any statements regarding Chandler Lumber prior to 1970. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: BURT HOWARD ET AL VS. DURO DYNE CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Notice Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for January 26, 2017, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 5. Defendant KB Home San Diego, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the basis for its motion warrants summary judgment. Defendant argues that plaintiffs cannot support a claim for premises/contractor liability pursuant to Privette v. Superior Court (1993) 5 Cal.4th 689 and its progeny. However even assuming that plaintiffs cannot support such a claim, defendant did not demonstrate that plaintiffs' Negligence II cause of action fails as plaintiffs' claims against defendant are based on the affirmative conduct of defendant's employees rather than the theories of liability articulated in Privette and the premises liability line of cases. Assuming that defendant properly raised the issue of whether there is evidence that defendant's employees exposed Mr. Howard to asbestos-containing products or materials, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence to support that contention. Mr. Howard's deposition testimony and plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from sustaining its burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
